summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn (PT) preached a powerful and personal message titled 'The Fire Is For You' on the two-year anniversary of surviving a life-threatening brain bleed. He opened by sharing his journey of recovery, describing it as taking the enemy's own weapon—the trial meant to destroy him—and using it for God's glory. The central theme is that the painful trials, or 'fires,' we face in life are not against us but are ultimately for our benefit and God's purpose. Pastor Travis provided five reasons why 'the fire is for you,' explaining that God uses pain as a teacher and warning light, pressure to transform and strengthen us, and suffering to purify our character. He emphasized that fires reveal God's presence, as seen when He walked with Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego, and that our pain produces our purpose, allowing us to comfort others with the comfort we have received. The sermon concluded with a call to receive the miracle of salvation through Jesus Christ, highlighting that our testimony, combined with the blood of the Lamb, is how we overcome.
mainPoints: The fire's purpose is pain: God uses painful experiences as a necessary teacher and a warning light to get our attention and change our ways.
The fire's purpose is pressure: God uses intense pressure, like a refiner's fire, to transform us, build our spiritual muscle, and produce maturity and purpose.
The fire produces purpose: Our deepest pains and trials are not meant to stop with us; they are to be stewarded so we can minister to others, turning our misery into ministry.

versesMentioned.text: Blows and wounds scrub away evil, and beatings purge the inmost being.

You know that under pressure, your faith-life is forced into the open and shows its true colors. So don’t try to get out of anything prematurely. Let it do its work so you become mature and well-developed, not deficient in any way.
We are hard pressed on every side, but not crushed; perplexed, but not in despair; persecuted, but not abandoned; struck down, but not destroyed.
As pressure and stress bear down on me, I find joy in your commands.
For you, God, tested us; you refined us like silver.
But he knows the way that I take; when he has tested me, I will come forth as gold.

These have come so that the proven genuineness of your faith—of greater worth than gold, which perishes even though refined by fire—may result in praise, glory and honor when Jesus Christ is revealed.
See, I have refined you, though not as silver; I have tested you in the furnace of suffering.

Now I am glad I sent it, not because it hurt you, but because the pain caused you to repent and change your ways. It was the kind of sorrow God wants his people to have, so you were not harmed by us in any way.

If we are thrown into the blazing furnace, the God whom we serve is able to save us. He will rescue us from your power, Your Majesty. But even if he doesn’t, we want to make it clear to you, Your Majesty, that we will never serve your gods or worship the gold statue you have set up.
They saw that the fire had not harmed their bodies, nor was a hair of their heads singed; their robes were not scorched, and there was no smell of fire on them.

When you pass through the waters, I will be with you; and when you pass through the rivers, they will not sweep over you. When you walk through the fire, you will not be burned; the flames will not set you ablaze.
who comforts us in all our troubles, so that we can comfort those in any trouble with the comfort we ourselves receive from God.
They triumphed over him by the blood of the Lamb and by the word of their testimony; they did not love their lives so much as to shrink from death.

If you declare with your mouth, 'Jesus is Lord,' and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved. For it is with your heart that you believe and are justified, and it is with your mouth that you profess your faith and are saved.

Monday.scripture: James 1:2-4 (NIV): 'Consider it pure joy, my brothers and sisters, whenever you face trials of many kinds, because you know that the testing of your faith produces perseverance.'
Monday.focus: Seeing trials as a gift that develops perseverance.
Monday.action: Identify one current difficulty and write down one way it could be strengthening your character.
Monday.prayer: Lord, help me to see my current trial through Your eyes. Give me the grace to count it as joy, trusting that You are using it to make me mature and complete.

Tuesday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 4:8-9 (NIV): 'We are hard pressed on every side, but not crushed; perplexed, but not in despair.'
Tuesday.focus: Trusting God's sustaining power under pressure.
Tuesday.action: When you feel pressure today, pause and declare out loud: 'I am hard pressed, but not crushed.'
Tuesday.prayer: Father, when the pressures of life feel overwhelming, remind me that You are with me. I am not crushed, abandoned, or destroyed because You hold me up.

Wednesday.scripture: Psalm 66:10 (NIV): 'For you, God, tested us; you refined us like silver.'
Wednesday.focus: Embracing God's refining process in our lives.
Wednesday.action: Reflect on a past 'fire' you've been through. Thank God for one way it made you stronger or brought you closer to Him.
Wednesday.prayer: Refiner, thank You for loving me enough to purify me. Help me to submit to Your refining fire, trusting that You are removing impurities to make me more like You.

Thursday.scripture: Daniel 3:17-18 (NLT): 'But even if he doesn’t, we want to make it clear to you... that we will never serve your gods.'
Thursday.focus: Cultivating unwavering faith, regardless of the outcome.
Thursday.action: Make a decision today to stand firm in one area of your faith, even if the immediate result is uncertain.
Thursday.prayer: God, my faith is in You alone. Even if You don't answer my prayer the way I hope, I choose to trust and obey You. I will not bow to fear or compromise.

Friday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 1:4 (NIV): '...so that we can comfort those in any trouble with the comfort we ourselves receive from God.'
Friday.focus: Stewarding our pain to minister to others.
Friday.action: Reach out to one person who is going through a hard time you have personally experienced. Offer a word of empathy and hope.
Friday.prayer: Comforting God, thank You for the comfort You've given me. Show me someone today who needs the same comfort. Use my story to bring them hope and point them to You.

Saturday.scripture: Revelation 12:11 (NIV): 'They triumphed over him by the blood of the Lamb and by the word of their testimony.'
Saturday.focus: The power of our testimony in overcoming the enemy.
Saturday.action: Share a brief part of your faith story with a friend or family member, focusing on what God has done for you.
Saturday.prayer: Jesus, thank You for the blood of the Lamb that saves me. Give me courage to share the word of my testimony, that others may see Your power and overcome.
